text.title: Design Decision Log Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design Decision Log Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: The log is append-only; superseded rows are NEVER edited. A superseding row references the prior row id and the prior row's status flips to 'superseded-by-NNN'.
rule.rationale: Editing accepted rows destroys the historical record needed to understand the path that produced the current design.

rule.statement: Decision text starts with 'We will' or 'We chose' in active voice; passive phrasing ('it was decided') is rejected.
rule.rationale: Active voice forces a clear actor and choice; passive voice hides the decision behind ambiguity.

rule.statement: Each row carries at least 2 considered alternatives with a one-line rejection reason; single-option rows are rejected (use a no-decision-yet status instead).
rule.rationale: Without alternatives the decision is unfalsifiable; the same debate recurs in week 4.

rule.statement: Every row carries the original thread / Figma / standup link so reviewers can audit the original context; prose-only context is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untraceable rows become folklore; the link lets future readers reconstruct what was actually said.

rule.statement: Owner field is a named handle; row carries last_reviewed date. Stale rows (>180 days unreviewed) surface a warning on read.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; dated review forces refresh cadence on the bank.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-decision-log-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
